findings.evidence: mapPoints is defined via GrpCat.ofHom (line 58); Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Basic.lean:156 gives @[simp] lemma hom_ofHom: (ofHom f).hom = f, so .hom.map_one is directly available.
findings.fix: Remove the lemma. The user accesses the property via (mapPoints φ).hom.map_one.
findings.issue: mapPoints_one is a trivial wrapper around (mapPoints φ).hom.map_one; it carries no @[simp] annotation and adds unnecessary API surface.

findings.evidence: Same as mapPoints_one — the proof body is exactly (mapPoints φ).hom.map_mul f g.
findings.issue: mapPoints_mul is a trivial wrapper around (mapPoints φ).hom.map_mul; it carries no @[simp] annotation and adds unnecessary API surface.

findings.evidence: Same pattern — the proof body is exactly (mapPoints φ).hom.map_inv f.
findings.issue: mapPoints_inv is a trivial wrapper around (mapPoints φ).hom.map_inv; it carries no @[simp] annotation and adds unnecessary API surface.

findings.evidence: The lemma is the normal form for the functor's object part; without @[simp] the characteristic API is incomplete for automation.
findings.issue: pointsFunctor_obj characterizes the functor's object action but lacks @[simp], so simp cannot normalize (pointsFunctor …).obj A.

findings.evidence: The lemma is the normal form for the functor's morphism part; analogous to mapPoints_apply which already carries @[simp].
findings.issue: pointsFunctor_map characterizes the functor's morphism action but lacks @[simp], so simp cannot normalize (pointsFunctor …).map φ.

summary: Three trivial convenience lemmas (mapPoints_one/mul/inv) are redundant — each is exactly (mapPoints φ).hom.map_one/map_mul/map_inv — and should be removed. The characteristic API lemmas pointsFunctor_obj and pointsFunctor_map are missing @[simp].
